calendar.matters:
Verified Petition For Instructions
Off Calendar Apr-03-2017 Off Calendar

Complaint For Conversion After Rejection Of Creditors Claim
Off Calendar Apr-03-2017 Off Calendar

Verified Petition For Instructions
Off Calendar Jan-30-2017 Continued To Apr-03-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: The Estate of Daniel. A. Lagan PES-15-299358 and The Daniel A. & Joanna A. Lagan Revocable Trust PTR-16-299549.

Appearances: Stephen Brooks for Debra "CeCe" Calli.

Mr. Brooks stated that Attorneys Jeffrey Lowe and Sarah Sheppard agree to a continuance, that the only thing remaining from their side is the issue of the US Savings Bonds and that they are working with the Treasury. The Petition for Letters of Administration, Petition for Instructions, and Complaint for Conversion After Rejection of Creditor's Claim are continued to April 3, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: D. Duong. Not reported.

Complaint For Conversion After Rejection Of Creditors Claim
Off Calendar Jan-30-2017 Continued To Apr-03-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Verified Petition For Instructions
Off Calendar Oct-26-2016 Continued To Jan-30-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Complaint For Conversion After Rejection Of Creditors Claim
Off Calendar Oct-26-2016 Continued To Jan-30-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Approval Of Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Sep-28-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jeffrey Lowe for (petitioner) Kamala Murphey. Stephen Brooks & Richard J. Wall for (respondent) Debra "CeCe" Calli. Ron Meyn (interested Party) in Propria Persona.

The Court stated that it has looked at the petition and the settlement agreement. Mr. Lowe stated that they litigated/negotiated for 9 months, that Ms. Calli has received the assets she was to receive and his client has received the assets of the Daniel & Joan trusts. Mr. Brooks stated that he echo's what Mr. Lowe said, that the settlement is fair and that they submit that the petition should be granted. Mr. Meyn stated that his sister "CeCe" was in agreement and represented them, that he has concerns regarding the settlement agreement, that on April 20th Mr. Brooks filed a creditors claim for $361,000.00, that it appears now that there is only $265,000.00 in the trust, that he had questions regarding the settlement agreement but no one would provide information, that without the information he has concerns, that it seems that there is money that was left to "CeCe" that should have been left to all of them and that it appears that $100,000.00 is missing from his grandfathers trust. Mr. Lowe stated that in terms of the dollar amounts the information was provided in the pleadings, that this was a long and hard fought process, that Mr. Meyn is late to the party, that Ms. Calli has agreed to fund the trusts and that they ask the Court to approve the agreement which was reached with the solicitation of all parties. Mr. Brooks stated that Mr. Meyn has not filed objections or responses, that he was given notice on all of the petitions, that he has not been a party, that "CeCe" is the successor trustee and that the other siblings have signed the settlement agreement. The Court took the Petition for Approval of Settlement Agreement under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Chan. (Not Reported)
